## 📝 Summary

<!--
Provide a concise summary of what this pull request is addressing.

If this PR fixes any issues, list them here by number (e.g., Fixes #123).
-->
This pull request introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

Fixes #5523

## 🔍 Description of Changes

<!--
Detail the specific changes made in this pull request. Explain the problem addressed and how it was resolved. If applicable, provide before and after comparisons, screenshots, or any relevant details to help reviewers understand the changes easily.
-->

Currently, when marimo export is used with an --output argument that points to an existing file, it interactively prompts the user for confirmation before overwriting. While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:

Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).

Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.

Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:

Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.

Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.

Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

I know it is not perfect, please @dmadisetti @mscolnick check this one out!
## 📋 Checklist

- [x] I have read the [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] For large changes, or changes that affect the public API: this change was discussed or approved through an issue, on [Discord](https://marimo.io/discord?ref=pr), or the community [discussions](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/discussions) (Please provide a link if applicable).
- [x] I have added tests for the changes made.
- [x] I have run the code and verified that it works as expected.

You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

A reactive Python notebook that's reproducible, git-friendly, and deployable as scripts or apps.

Docs · Discord · Examples · Gallery · YouTube

English | 简体中文 | 日本語 | Español

**marimo** is a reactive Python notebook: run a cell or interact with a UI element, and marimo automatically runs dependent cells (or marks them as stale), keeping code and outputs consistent. marimo notebooks are stored as pure Python (with first-class SQL support), executable as scripts, and deployable as apps. **Highlights**. - 🚀 **batteries-included:** replaces `jupyter`, `streamlit`, `jupytext`, `ipywidgets`, `papermill`, and more - ⚡️ **reactive**: run a cell, and marimo reactively [runs all dependent cells](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/reactivity.html) or marks them as stale - 🖐️ **interactive:** [bind sliders, tables, plots, and more](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/interactivity.html) to Python — no callbacks required - 🐍 **git-friendly:** stored as `.py` files - 🛢️ **designed for data**: query dataframes, databases, warehouses, or lakehouses [with SQL](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/working_with_data/sql.html), filter and search [dataframes](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/working_with_data/dataframes.html) - 🤖 **AI-native**: [generate cells with AI](ht ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

## Description
## 📝 Summary

<!--
Provide a concise summary of what this pull request is addressing.

If this PR fixes any issues, list them here by number (e.g., Fixes #123).
-->
This pull request introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:

Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).

Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.

Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:

Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.

Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.

Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file defines the CLI "export" command group and several subcommands (html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm) for exporting marimo notebooks to different formats. It also implements the central watch_and_export logic used by those subcommands to handle writing output, prompting before overwriting, and optional file watching.

### Architecture
- Top-level click group: `export` collects subcommands.
- Subcommands (`html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, `html_wasm`) are Click command functions that:
  - parse options/flags,
  - optionally invoke sandbox logic (`maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox`, `run_in_sandbox`),
  - construct an `export_callback` that returns an `ExportResult`,
  - then call `watch_and_export` with a `MarimoPath` and the callback.
- Shared logic: `watch_and_export` handles output writing, overwrite prompting via `prompt_to_overwrite`, non-watch runs, and a watch mode that uses `FileWatcher` to re-run the export on file changes.
- Auxiliary functionality used by commands:
  - Export routines: `export_as_script`, `export_as_md`, `export_as_ipynb`, `export_as_wasm`, `run_app_then_export_as_html`, `run_app_then_export_as_ipynb`.
  - Utilities: `parse_args`, `echo`, `green`, `maybe_make_dirs`, `parse_title`, `create_cloudflare_files`, `asyncio_run`.
  - Dependency management: `DependencyManager.nbformat.require`.
  - Asset management: `Exporter().export_assets`, `Exporter().export_public_folder`.

### Functions & Classes
- `_watch_message` — module-level string describing the watch behavior shown in help text. Important for CLI help descriptions.
- `_sandbox_message` — module-level string describing sandbox behavior shown in help text. Important for CLI help descriptions.

- `export` — Click group function declared with `@click.group(...)`. Collects and exposes the `html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, and `html_wasm` subcommands. Importance: the root of the export CLI namespace.

- `watch_and_export(marimo_path: MarimoPath, output: Optional[Path], watch: bool, export_callback: Callable[[MarimoPath], ExportResult]) -> None` — central helper that:
  - Validates that `--watch` cannot be used without `--output` (raises `click.UsageError`).
  - Defines nested helper `write_data`, `on_file_changed`, and `start`.
  - Uses `prompt_to_overwrite` to confirm before overwriting an existing output file (calls `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` and returns if the user declines).
  - If not watching: calls `export_callback(marimo_path)`, writes `result.contents` via `write_data`, and raises `click.ClickException` if `result.did_error` is True.
  - If watching: sets up an async loop using `FileWatcher.create(marimo_path.path, on_file_changed)`; prints watching message and re-runs export on file changes; runs an infinite async loop until KeyboardInterrupt and stops the watcher.
  - Relies on `asyncio_run` to run the async watcher startup.
  Importance: This function implements overwrite prompting, output writing (to file or stdout), and the entire watch behavior used by all export subcommands.

  - Nested `write_data(data: str) -> None` — writes `data` to `output` if provided (ensuring directories via `maybe_make_dirs`), otherwise prints `data` using `echo`. Importance: centralizes how export output is delivered.
  - Nested `on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None` (async) — callback passed to `FileWatcher.create`; prints a re-exporting message and calls `export_callback` for the changed file, then writes out the new contents. Importance: handles per-change re-export behavior.
  - Nested `start() -> None` (async) — creates and starts the `FileWatcher`, prints the watching notification, and awaits in a loop until KeyboardInterrupt, then stops the watcher. Importance: lifecycle management for file watching.

- `html(name: str, include_code: bool, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool], args: tuple[str]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--include-code/--no-include-code`, `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`, argument `name`, trailing `args`.
  - Behavior:
    - If `sandbox` is None, calls `maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox(name)` to set default.
    - If `sandbox` True, calls `run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name)` and returns.
    - Parses CLI args for the notebook via `parse_args(args)`.
    - Builds `export_callback` that wraps `run_app_then_export_as_html` executed via `asyncio_run`, passing `include_code`, `cli_args`, and `argv=list(args)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  Importance: Implements "marimo export html" which executes the app and exports an HTML snapshot (optionally including code), and supports sandboxing, watch mode, and CLI args passed to the notebook.

- `script(name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - Sandbox behavior analogous to `html`.
    - `export_callback` returns `export_as_script(file_path)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Exports the notebook as a flattened Python script in topological order. Supports watch mode and sandboxing.

- `md(name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - Sandbox behavior analogous to `html`.
    - `export_callback` returns `export_as_md(file_path, new_filename=output)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Exports the notebook as a code-fenced Markdown document. Supports watch mode and sandboxing.

- `ipynb(name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sort: Literal["top-down", "topological"], include_outputs: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--sort` (choice "top-down" or "topological"), `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--include-outputs`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - If `include_outputs` is True, possibly prompts for sandbox and runs `run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name, additional_deps=["nbformat"])` if sandbox chosen.
    - Calls `DependencyManager.nbformat.require(why="to convert marimo notebooks to ipynb")` (ensures nbformat is available).
    - Constructs `export_callback`:
      - If `include_outputs` True -> runs `run_app_then_export_as_ipynb` via `asyncio_run` with `sort_mode=sort`.
      - Else -> calls `export_as_ipynb(file_path, sort_mode=sort)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Exports to Jupyter notebook format; when `--include-outputs` is used it runs the notebook to capture outputs. Ensures `nbformat` dependency and supports watch and sandboxing.

- `html_wasm(name: str, output: Path, mode: Literal["edit", "run"], watch: bool, show_code: bool, include_cloudflare: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `-o/--output` (required), `--mode` (required; choice "edit" or "run"), `--watch`, `--show-code`, `--include-cloudflare`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - Sandbox handling analogous to other commands.
    - If provided `output` has suffix `.html`, it sets `out_dir = output.parent`, `filename = output.name`, and `ignore_index_html = True`; otherwise `out_dir = output`, `filename = "index.html"`.
    - Creates `marimo_file = MarimoPath(name)`.
    - `export_callback` calls `export_as_wasm(file_path, mode, show_code=show_code)`.
    - Calls `Exporter().export_assets(out_dir, ignore_index_html=ignore_index_html)` to copy static assets required for WASM export.
    - Touches (creates) `(Path(out_dir) / ".nojekyll")` to avoid GitHub Pages interfering.
    - Calls `Exporter().export_public_folder(out_dir, marimo_file)`; prints message if public folder copied.
    - If `include_cloudflare` True: calls `create_cloudflare_files(parse_title(name), out_dir)`.
    - Computes `outfile = out_dir / filename` and passes that to `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Produces a self-contained HTML that runs via WebAssembly (Pyodide), copies assets and public folder, and can optionally emit Cloudflare Worker files for deployment. Uses `watch_and_export` for writing the generated HTML and for optional watch behaviour. Notable: `-o/--output` is required here (enforced by Click).

- Calls at bottom:
  - `export.add_command(html)`
  - `export.add_command(script)`
  - `export.add_command(md)`
  - `export.add_command(ipynb)`
  - `export.add_command(html_wasm)`
  Importance: attaches the subcommands to the `export` group so they're available on the CLI.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is looking for:
- Command definitions for export subcommands: found as exact click command functions `html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, and `html_wasm`. These functions define flags and options for each export mode (e.g., `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`, `--include-outputs`, `--mode`, `--show-code`, `--include-cloudflare`, `--include-code`).
- Force flag addition: There is no `--force` flag implemented in this file. Instead, `watch_and_export` uses `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` to prompt interactively before overwriting an existing output and returns if the user declines. If you plan to add a `--force` flag, you would modify the subcommands' Click options and the `watch_and_export` behavior to bypass `prompt_to_overwrite` when `force` is True.

# Copyright 2024 Marimo. All rights reserved.
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Callable, Literal, Optional

import click

from marimo._cli.export.cloudflare import create_cloudflare_files
from marimo._cli.parse_args import parse_args
from marimo._cli.print import echo, green
from marimo._cli.utils import prompt_to_overwrite
from marimo._dependencies.dependencies import DependencyManager
from marimo._server.api.utils import parse_title
from marimo._server.export import (
    ExportResult,
    export_as_ipynb,
    export_as_md,
    export_as_script,
    export_as_wasm,
    run_app_then_export_as_html,
    run_app_then_export_as_ipynb,
)
from marimo._server.export.exporter import Exporter
from marimo._server.utils import asyncio_run
from marimo._utils.file_watcher import FileWatcher
from marimo._utils.marimo_path import MarimoPath
from marimo._utils.paths import maybe_make_dirs

_watch_message = (
    "Watch notebook for changes and regenerate the output on modification. "
    "If watchdog is installed, it will be used to watch the file. "
    "Otherwise, file watcher will poll the file every 1s."
)

_sandbox_message = (
    "Run the command in an isolated virtual environment using "
    "`uv run --isolated`. Requires `uv`."
)


@click.group(help="""Export a notebook to various formats.""")
def export() -> None:
    pass


def watch_and_export(
    marimo_path: MarimoPath,
    output: Optional[Path],
    watch: bool,
    export_callback: Callable[[MarimoPath], ExportResult],
) -> None:
    if watch and not output:
        raise click.UsageError(
            "Cannot use --watch without providing "
            + "an output file with --output."
        )

    def write_data(data: str) -> None:
        if output:
            # Make dirs if needed
            maybe_make_dirs(output)
            output.write_text(data, encoding="utf-8")
        else:
            echo(data)
        return

    if output:
        output_path = Path(output)
        if not prompt_to_overwrite(output_path):
            return

    # No watch, just run once
    if not watch:
        result = export_callback(marimo_path)
        write_data(result.contents)
        if result.did_error:
            raise click.ClickException(
                "Export was successful, but some cells failed to execute."
            )
        return

    async def on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None:
        if output:
            echo(
                f"File {str(file_path)} changed. Re-exporting to {green(str(output))}"
            )
        result = export_callback(MarimoPath(file_path))
        write_data(result.contents)

    async def start() -> None:
        # Watch the file for changes
        watcher = FileWatcher.create(marimo_path.path, on_file_changed)
        echo(f"Watching {green(marimo_path.relative_name)} for changes...")
        watcher.start()
        try:
            # Run forever
            while True:  # noqa: ASYNC110
                await asyncio.sleep(1)
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            watcher.stop()

    asyncio_run(start())


@click.command(

@click.command(
    help="""Export a notebook as a WASM-powered standalone HTML file.

Example:

    marimo export html-wasm notebook.py -o notebook.wasm.html

The exported HTML file will run the notebook using WebAssembly, making it
completely self-contained and executable in the browser. This lets you
share interactive notebooks on the web without setting up
infrastructure to run Python code.

The exported notebook runs using Pyodide, which supports most
but not all Python packages. To learn more, see the Pyodide
documentation.

In order for this file to be able to run, it must be served over HTTP,
and cannot be opened directly from the file system (e.g. file://).
"""
)
@click.option(
    "-o",
    "--output",
    type=click.Path(path_type=Path),
    required=True,
    help="Output directory to save the HTML to.",
)
@click.option(
    "--mode",
    type=click.Choice(["edit", "run"]),
    default="run",
    help="Whether the notebook code should be editable or readonly.",
    show_default=True,
    required=True,
)
@click.option(
    "--watch/--no-watch",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    help=("Whether to watch the original file and export upon change"),
)
@click.option(
    "--show-code/--no-show-code",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    help=(
        "Whether to show code by default in the exported HTML file; "
        "only relevant for run mode."
    ),
)
@click.option(
    "--include-cloudflare/--no-include-cloudflare",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    help=(
        "Whether to include Cloudflare Worker configuration files"
        " (index.js and wrangler.jsonc) for easy deployment."
    ),
)
@click.option(
    "--sandbox/--no-sandbox",
    is_flag=True,
    default=None,
    show_default=False,
    type=bool,
    help=_sandbox_message,
)
@click.argument(
    "name",
    required=True,
    type=click.Path(exists=True, file_okay=True, dir_okay=False),
)
def html_wasm(
    name: str,
    output: Path,
    mode: Literal["edit", "run"],
    watch: bool,
    show_code: bool,
    include_cloudflare: bool,
    sandbox: Optional[bool],
) -> None:
    """Export a notebook as a WASM-powered standalone HTML file."""
    import sys

    # Set default, if not provided
    if sandbox is None:
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox

        sandbox = maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox(name)

    if sandbox:
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import run_in_sandbox

        run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name)
        return

    out_dir = output
    filename = "index.html"
    ignore_index_html = False
    # If ends with .html, get the directory
    if output.suffix == ".html":
        out_dir = output.parent
        filename = output.name
        ignore_index_html = True

    marimo_file = MarimoPath(name)

    def export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult:
        return export_as_wasm(file_path, mode, show_code=show_code)

    # Export assets first
    Exporter().export_assets(out_dir, ignore_index_html=ignore_index_html)

    # Create .nojekyll file to prevent GitHub Pages from interfering with asset
    # resolution
    (Path(out_dir) / ".nojekyll").touch()

    echo(
        f"Assets copied to {green(str(out_dir))}. These assets are required for the "
        "notebook to run in the browser."
    )

    did_export_public = Exporter().export_public_folder(out_dir, marimo_file)
    if did_export_public:
        echo(
            f"The public folder next to your notebook was copied to "
            f"{green(str(out_dir))}."
        )

    echo(
        "To run the exported notebook, use:\n"
        f"  python -m http.server --directory {out_dir}\n"
        "Then open the URL that is printed to your terminal."
    )

    if include_cloudflare:
        create_cloudflare_files(parse_title(name), out_dir)

    outfile = out_dir / filename
    return watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), outfile, watch, export_callback)

summarization.summary:
### File Overview
This file defines the CLI "export" command group and several subcommands (html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm) for exporting marimo notebooks to different formats. It also implements the central watch_and_export logic used by those subcommands to handle writing output, prompting before overwriting, and optional file watching.

### Architecture
- Top-level click group: `export` collects subcommands.
- Subcommands (`html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, `html_wasm`) are Click command functions that:
  - parse options/flags,
  - optionally invoke sandbox logic (`maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox`, `run_in_sandbox`),
  - construct an `export_callback` that returns an `ExportResult`,
  - then call `watch_and_export` with a `MarimoPath` and the callback.
- Shared logic: `watch_and_export` handles output writing, overwrite prompting via `prompt_to_overwrite`, non-watch runs, and a watch mode that uses `FileWatcher` to re-run the export on file changes.
- Auxiliary functionality used by commands:
  - Export routines: `export_as_script`, `export_as_md`, `export_as_ipynb`, `export_as_wasm`, `run_app_then_export_as_html`, `run_app_then_export_as_ipynb`.
  - Utilities: `parse_args`, `echo`, `green`, `maybe_make_dirs`, `parse_title`, `create_cloudflare_files`, `asyncio_run`.
  - Dependency management: `DependencyManager.nbformat.require`.
  - Asset management: `Exporter().export_assets`, `Exporter().export_public_folder`.

### Functions & Classes
- `_watch_message` — module-level string describing the watch behavior shown in help text. Important for CLI help descriptions.
- `_sandbox_message` — module-level string describing sandbox behavior shown in help text. Important for CLI help descriptions.

- `export` — Click group function declared with `@click.group(...)`. Collects and exposes the `html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, and `html_wasm` subcommands. Importance: the root of the export CLI namespace.

- `watch_and_export(marimo_path: MarimoPath, output: Optional[Path], watch: bool, export_callback: Callable[[MarimoPath], ExportResult]) -> None` — central helper that:
  - Validates that `--watch` cannot be used without `--output` (raises `click.UsageError`).
  - Defines nested helper `write_data`, `on_file_changed`, and `start`.
  - Uses `prompt_to_overwrite` to confirm before overwriting an existing output file (calls `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` and returns if the user declines).
  - If not watching: calls `export_callback(marimo_path)`, writes `result.contents` via `write_data`, and raises `click.ClickException` if `result.did_error` is True.
  - If watching: sets up an async loop using `FileWatcher.create(marimo_path.path, on_file_changed)`; prints watching message and re-runs export on file changes; runs an infinite async loop until KeyboardInterrupt and stops the watcher.
  - Relies on `asyncio_run` to run the async watcher startup.
  Importance: This function implements overwrite prompting, output writing (to file or stdout), and the entire watch behavior used by all export subcommands.

  - Nested `write_data(data: str) -> None` — writes `data` to `output` if provided (ensuring directories via `maybe_make_dirs`), otherwise prints `data` using `echo`. Importance: centralizes how export output is delivered.
  - Nested `on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None` (async) — callback passed to `FileWatcher.create`; prints a re-exporting message and calls `export_callback` for the changed file, then writes out the new contents. Importance: handles per-change re-export behavior.
  - Nested `start() -> None` (async) — creates and starts the `FileWatcher`, prints the watching notification, and awaits in a loop until KeyboardInterrupt, then stops the watcher. Importance: lifecycle management for file watching.

- `html(name: str, include_code: bool, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool], args: tuple[str]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--include-code/--no-include-code`, `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`, argument `name`, trailing `args`.
  - Behavior:
    - If `sandbox` is None, calls `maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox(name)` to set default.
    - If `sandbox` True, calls `run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name)` and returns.
    - Parses CLI args for the notebook via `parse_args(args)`.
    - Builds `export_callback` that wraps `run_app_then_export_as_html` executed via `asyncio_run`, passing `include_code`, `cli_args`, and `argv=list(args)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  Importance: Implements "marimo export html" which executes the app and exports an HTML snapshot (optionally including code), and supports sandboxing, watch mode, and CLI args passed to the notebook.

- `script(name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - Sandbox behavior analogous to `html`.
    - `export_callback` returns `export_as_script(file_path)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Exports the notebook as a flattened Python script in topological order. Supports watch mode and sandboxing.

- `md(name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - Sandbox behavior analogous to `html`.
    - `export_callback` returns `export_as_md(file_path, new_filename=output)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Exports the notebook as a code-fenced Markdown document. Supports watch mode and sandboxing.

- `ipynb(name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sort: Literal["top-down", "topological"], include_outputs: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `--sort` (choice "top-down" or "topological"), `--watch`, `-o/--output`, `--include-outputs`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - If `include_outputs` is True, possibly prompts for sandbox and runs `run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name, additional_deps=["nbformat"])` if sandbox chosen.
    - Calls `DependencyManager.nbformat.require(why="to convert marimo notebooks to ipynb")` (ensures nbformat is available).
    - Constructs `export_callback`:
      - If `include_outputs` True -> runs `run_app_then_export_as_ipynb` via `asyncio_run` with `sort_mode=sort`.
      - Else -> calls `export_as_ipynb(file_path, sort_mode=sort)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export`.
  Importance: Exports to Jupyter notebook format; when `--include-outputs` is used it runs the notebook to capture outputs. Ensures `nbformat` dependency and supports watch and sandboxing.

- `html_wasm(name: str, output: Path, mode: Literal["edit", "run"], watch: bool, show_code: bool, include_cloudflare: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None` — Click command:
  - Options: `-o/--output` (required), `--mode` (required; choice "edit" or "run"), `--watch`, `--show-code`, `--include-cloudflare`, `--sandbox`.
  - Behavior:
    - Sandbox handling analogous to other commands.
    - If provided `output` has suffix `.html`, it sets `out_dir = output.parent`, `filename = output.name`, and `ignore_index_html = True`; otherwise `out_dir = output`, `filename = "index.html"`.
    - Creates `marimo_file = MarimoPath(name)`.
    - `export_callback` calls `export_as_wasm(file_path, mode, show_code=show_code)`.

### File Overview
This file defines the CLI "export" command group and several subcommands for exporting marimo notebooks to different formats (HTML, script, Markdown, Jupyter ipynb, and WASM-powered HTML). It centralizes export flow logic in watch_and_export, which writes export outputs, optionally prompts before overwriting, and can watch the source file for changes.

### Architecture
- Top-level click command group: `export` collects subcommands.
- Each subcommand (`html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, `html_wasm`) defines an inner `export_callback` function that produces an `ExportResult` for a given `MarimoPath`, then calls the shared `watch_and_export` function with that callback.
- `watch_and_export` encapsulates:
  - validation (e.g., cannot use `--watch` without `--output`),
  - an overwrite prompt via `prompt_to_overwrite`,
  - a writer (`write_data`) that either writes to `output` or echoes to stdout,
  - a file-watching flow using `FileWatcher.create` and an async loop (`start`) to re-run the `export_callback` when the source changes.
- `html_wasm` has additional preparatory steps (copying assets using `Exporter().export_assets`, creating a `.nojekyll` file, exporting public folder via `Exporter().export_public_folder`, and optionally creating Cloudflare files via `create_cloudflare_files`) before delegating to `watch_and_export`.

The file uses utilities imported from the codebase such as `MarimoPath`, `ExportResult`, `export_as_*` functions, `run_app_then_export_*` functions, `asyncio_run`, `FileWatcher`, `prompt_to_overwrite`, and CLI helpers like `echo` and `green`.

### Functions & Classes
- `export` — a click group decorated function (click.group). Purpose: top-level command group for all export subcommands. Importance: organizes subcommands under `marimo export`.

- `watch_and_export` — signature: (marimo_path: MarimoPath, output: Optional[Path], watch: bool, export_callback: Callable[[MarimoPath], ExportResult]) -> None.
  - Overall purpose: central routine that executes an export once or repeatedly when watching a file, and writes/prints the export output.
  - Key behavior:
    - Raises `click.UsageError` if `watch` is True but `output` is not provided (ensures watching requires an output file).
    - Defines inner function `write_data` to either write the export data to `output` (creating parent dirs with `maybe_make_dirs`) or echo to stdout.
    - If `output` is provided, constructs `output_path = Path(output)` and calls `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)`. If `prompt_to_overwrite` returns False, `watch_and_export` returns early (no export occurs).
    - If `watch` is False: calls `export_callback(marimo_path)`, writes `result.contents` via `write_data`, raises `click.ClickException` if `result.did_error` is True (indicating some cells failed to execute), then returns.
    - If `watch` is True:
      - Defines async inner `on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None` that logs re-exporting and calls `export_callback(MarimoPath(file_path))` and writes the new `result.contents`.
      - Defines async inner `start() -> None` that creates a watcher via `FileWatcher.create(marimo_path.path, on_file_changed)`, starts the watcher, prints watching message, and waits in an infinite asyncio.sleep loop until KeyboardInterrupt, upon which it stops the watcher.
      - Runs the `start()` coroutine via `asyncio_run(start())`.
  - Importance: This is the central location where the overwrite prompt (`prompt_to_overwrite`) is invoked and where a force/skip-overwrite flag would need to be handled if introduced.

- `write_data` — inner function inside `watch_and_export` (exact name `write_data`).
  - Purpose: writes the export `data` string to `output` (creating directories via `maybe_make_dirs`) or echoes it to stdout if no `output` provided.
  - Importance: handles final persistence of exported contents; used both for single-run exports and re-exports during watch.

- `on_file_changed` — inner async function inside `watch_and_export` (exact name `on_file_changed`).
  - Signature: async def on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None
  - Purpose: callback invoked by the file watcher when the source file changes; logs and re-runs the `export_callback` for the changed file and writes the new contents via `write_data`.
  - Importance: drives re-export behavior when `--watch` is used.

- `start` — inner async function inside `watch_and_export` (exact name `start`).
  - Purpose: creates and starts the `FileWatcher` with `on_file_changed`, prints a watching message, and runs an infinite loop until KeyboardInterrupt to keep the CLI process alive while watching.
  - Importance: coordinates the lifetime of the file watcher and ensures the CLI remains responsive to file-change events.

- `html` — click command function: signature (name: str, include_code: bool, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool], args: tuple[str]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Run a notebook and export it as an HTML file."
  - Key behavior:
    - May prompt to run in a sandbox via `maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox` if `sandbox` is None; if `sandbox` True, calls `run_in_sandbox`.
    - Parses extra CLI args via `parse_args(args)`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that calls `asyncio_run(run_app_then_export_as_html(...))`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: demonstrates how `export_callback` can run the app and generate HTML asynchronously; shows how `watch_and_export` is invoked for HTML exports.

- `script` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a marimo notebook as a flat script, in topological order."
  - Key behavior:
    - Optionally handles sandboxing similar to `html`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that returns `export_as_script(file_path)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: shows a simple synchronous `export_callback` that directly calls `export_as_script`.

- `md` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a marimo notebook as a code fenced Markdown file."
  - Key behavior:
    - Optionally handles sandboxing.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that returns `export_as_md(file_path, new_filename=output)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: illustrates passing `output` into the exporter (`new_filename=output`) and delegating to `watch_and_export`.

- `ipynb` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sort: Literal["top-down", "topological"], include_outputs: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a marimo notebook as a Jupyter notebook in topological order."
  - Key behavior:
    - If `include_outputs` True, may prompt/perform sandboxing and may call `run_in_sandbox` with additional_deps `["nbformat"]`.
    - Requires `nbformat` via `DependencyManager.nbformat.require(...)`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult`:
      - If `include_outputs` True: returns `asyncio_run(run_app_then_export_as_ipynb(...))`.
      - Else: returns `export_as_ipynb(file_path, sort_mode=sort)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: shows conditional use of an async export path (when including outputs) vs synchronous exporter, and again delegates overwrite prompting and watching to `watch_and_export`.

- `html_wasm` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, mode: Literal["edit", "run"], watch: bool, show_code: bool, include_cloudflare: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a notebook as a WASM-powered standalone HTML file."
  - Key behavior:
    - Optional sandbox prompt/run similar to other commands.
    - Determines `out_dir`, `filename`, and `ignore_index_html` depending on whether `output` ends with `.html`.
    - Prepares the output directory:
      - Calls `Exporter().export_assets(out_dir, ignore_index_html=ignore_index_html)`.
      - Creates a `.nojekyll` file in `out_dir`.
      - Logs asset copy messages via `echo` and `green`.
      - Calls `Exporter().export_public_folder(out_dir, marimo_file)` and logs if public folder copied.
      - If `include_cloudflare` True: calls `create_cloudflare_files(parse_title(name), out_dir)`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that returns `export_as_wasm(file_path, mode, show_code=show_code)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), outfile, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: demonstrates pre-export asset copying and other side effects that occur before calling `watch_and_export`, and shows that `watch_and_export` will be used for re-exporting the main HTML file (the output passed is a concrete file path).

- `Exporter().export_assets` — method called in `html_wasm`. Purpose/importance here: copies required static assets to the output directory prior to exporting WASM HTML. (Referenced by exact name in code: `Exporter().export_assets`.)

- `Exporter().export_public_folder` — method called in `html_wasm`. Purpose/importance here: optionally copies a "public" folder next to the notebook into the output directory; the boolean return is checked and logged.

- `FileWatcher.create` — function/classmethod used in `watch_and_export` to create a watcher for the source path with callback `on_file_changed`. Importance: the mechanism used to watch file changes for `--watch`.

- `prompt_to_overwrite` — function imported from `marimo._cli.utils` and invoked in `watch_and_export` as `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)`.
  - Purpose/importance in this file: If `output` is provided, `watch_and_export` calls `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` and immediately returns (aborts the export) if it returns False. This is the single centralized prompt-before-overwrite behavior for all export commands in this file. Any change to add a `--force` flag to skip the prompt will need to alter how/where `prompt_to_overwrite` is invoked (e.g., add a flag parameter to `watch_and_export` and conditionally skip calling `prompt_to_overwrite`).

- Other referenced functions/utilities used here (exact names as used in file):
  - `maybe_make_dirs` — ensures output directories exist before writing files (used in `write_data`).
  - `echo`, `green` — CLI printing utilities used for messages.
  - `asyncio_run` — used to run async exporter operations and the watcher `start()` coroutine.
  - `run_app_then_export_as_html`, `run_app_then_export_as_ipynb`, `export_as_ipynb`, `export_as_md`, `export_as_script`, `export_as_wasm` — exporter functions used by various `export_callback` implementations.
  - `parse_args` — used in `html` to parse CLI args passed after `--`.
  - `create_cloudflare_files`, `parse_title` — used in `html_wasm` when `include_cloudflare` is True.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent wants to inspect `watch_and_export` and the usage of `prompt_to_overwrite` to plan modifications to accept a force flag. Key facts from this file that are relevant:
- `prompt_to_overwrite` is called exactly once in the file inside `watch_and_export` when `output` is truthy:
  - Code pattern: 
    - `if output:` -> `output_path = Path(output)` -> `if not prompt_to_overwrite(output_path): return`
- Because all export subcommands call `watch_and_export(...)`, adding a global `--force`/`--yes` style flag to skip overwrite prompts should be implemented by:
  - Adding a parameter (e.g., `force: bool`) to `watch_and_export`, and
  - Passing that parameter from each command (by adding a click option to the commands), and
  - Changing the prompt invocation to be conditional: only call `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` when `force` is False.
- The prompt is checked before any export (and before watch logic); the current behavior prevents any export if the user declines the prompt. Any change should preserve that guard unless `force` indicates to skip it.
- The watch-related logic is decoupled from prompting: `prompt_to_overwrite` is invoked once up front for the provided `output`, regardless of `watch`. So the force flag should be threaded through to `watch_and_export` rather than trying to modify each exporter callback.

This file is the correct central place to implement the force flag to bypass overwrite prompts for all export subcommands.

# Copyright 2024 Marimo. All rights reserved.
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Callable, Literal, Optional

import click

from marimo._cli.export.cloudflare import create_cloudflare_files
from marimo._cli.parse_args import parse_args
from marimo._cli.print import echo, green
from marimo._cli.utils import prompt_to_overwrite
from marimo._dependencies.dependencies import DependencyManager
from marimo._server.api.utils import parse_title
from marimo._server.export import (
    ExportResult,
    export_as_ipynb,
    export_as_md,
    export_as_script,
    export_as_wasm,
    run_app_then_export_as_html,
    run_app_then_export_as_ipynb,
)
from marimo._server.export.exporter import Exporter
from marimo._server.utils import asyncio_run
from marimo._utils.file_watcher import FileWatcher
from marimo._utils.marimo_path import MarimoPath
from marimo._utils.paths import maybe_make_dirs

_watch_message = (
    "Watch notebook for changes and regenerate the output on modification. "
    "If watchdog is installed, it will be used to watch the file. "
    "Otherwise, file watcher will poll the file every 1s."
)

_sandbox_message = (
    "Run the command in an isolated virtual environment using "
    "`uv run --isolated`. Requires `uv`."
)


@click.group(help="""Export a notebook to various formats.""")
def export() -> None:
    pass


def watch_and_export(
    marimo_path: MarimoPath,
    output: Optional[Path],
    watch: bool,
    export_callback: Callable[[MarimoPath], ExportResult],
) -> None:
    if watch and not output:
        raise click.UsageError(
            "Cannot use --watch without providing "
            + "an output file with --output."
        )

    def write_data(data: str) -> None:
        if output:
            # Make dirs if needed
            maybe_make_dirs(output)
            output.write_text(data, encoding="utf-8")
        else:
            echo(data)
        return

    if output:
        output_path = Path(output)
        if not prompt_to_overwrite(output_path):
            return

    # No watch, just run once
    if not watch:
        result = export_callback(marimo_path)
        write_data(result.contents)
        if result.did_error:
            raise click.ClickException(
                "Export was successful, but some cells failed to execute."
            )
        return

    async def on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None:
        if output:
            echo(
                f"File {str(file_path)} changed. Re-exporting to {green(str(output))}"
            )
        result = export_callback(MarimoPath(file_path))
        write_data(result.contents)

    async def start() -> None:
        # Watch the file for changes
        watcher = FileWatcher.create(marimo_path.path, on_file_changed)
        echo(f"Watching {green(marimo_path.relative_name)} for changes...")
        watcher.start()
        try:
            # Run forever
            while True:  # noqa: ASYNC110
                await asyncio.sleep(1)
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            watcher.stop()

    asyncio_run(start())


@click.command(
    help="""Run a notebook and export it as an HTML file.

Example:

    marimo export html notebook.py -o notebook.html

Optionally pass CLI args to the notebook:

    marimo export html notebook.py -o notebook.html -- -arg1 foo -arg2 bar
"""
)
@click.option(
    "--include-code/--no-include-code",
    default=True,
    show_default=True,
    type=bool,
    help="Include notebook code in the exported HTML file.",
)
@click.option(
    "--watch/--no-watch",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    type=bool,
    help=_watch_message,
)
@click.option(
    "-o",
    "--output",
    type=click.Path(path_type=Path),
    default=None,
    help=(
        "Output file to save the HTML to. "
        "If not provided, the HTML will be printed to stdout."
    ),
)
@click.option(
    "--sandbox/--no-sandbox",
    is_flag=True,
    default=None,
    show_default=False,
    type=bool,
    help=_sandbox_message,
)
@click.argument(
    "name",
    required=True,
    type=click.Path(exists=True, file_okay=True, dir_okay=False),
)
@click.argument("args", nargs=-1, type=click.UNPROCESSED)
def html(
    name: str,
    include_code: bool,
    output: Path,
    watch: bool,
    sandbox: Optional[bool],
    args: tuple[str],
) -> None:
    """Run a notebook and export it as an HTML file."""
    import sys

    # Set default, if not provided
    if sandbox is None:
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox

        sandbox = maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox(name)

    if sandbox:
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import run_in_sandbox

        run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name)
        return

    cli_args = parse_args(args)

    def export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult:
        return asyncio_run(
            run_app_then_export_as_html(
                file_path,
                include_code=include_code,
                cli_args=cli_args,
                argv=list(args),

    return watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)

    type=click.Path(path_type=Path),
    default=None,
    help=(
        "Output file to save the ipynb file to. "
        "If not provided, the ipynb contents will be printed to stdout."
    ),
)
@click.option(
    "--include-outputs/--no-include-outputs",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    type=bool,
    help="Run the notebook and include outputs in the exported ipynb file.",
)
@click.option(
    "--sandbox/--no-sandbox",
    is_flag=True,
    default=None,
    show_default=False,
    type=bool,
    help=_sandbox_message,
)
@click.argument(
    "name",
    required=True,
    type=click.Path(exists=True, file_okay=True, dir_okay=False),
)
def ipynb(
    name: str,
    output: Path,
    watch: bool,
    sort: Literal["top-down", "topological"],
    include_outputs: bool,
    sandbox: Optional[bool],
) -> None:
    """
    Export a marimo notebook as a Jupyter notebook in topological order.
    """
    import sys

    if include_outputs:
        # Set default, if not provided
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox

        if sandbox is None:
            sandbox = maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox(name)

        if sandbox:
            from marimo._cli.sandbox import run_in_sandbox

            run_in_sandbox(
                sys.argv[1:],
                name=name,
                additional_deps=["nbformat"],
            )
            return

    DependencyManager.nbformat.require(
        why="to convert marimo notebooks to ipynb"
    )

    def export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult:
        if include_outputs:
            return asyncio_run(
                run_app_then_export_as_ipynb(
                    file_path,
                    sort_mode=sort,
                    cli_args={},
                    argv=None,
                )
            )
        return export_as_ipynb(file_path, sort_mode=sort)

    return watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)


@click.command(
    help="""Export a notebook as a WASM-powered standalone HTML file.

Example:

    marimo export html-wasm notebook.py -o notebook.wasm.html

The exported HTML file will run the notebook using WebAssembly, making it
completely self-contained and executable in the browser. This lets you
share interactive notebooks on the web without setting up
infrastructure to run Python code.

The exported notebook runs using Pyodide, which supports most
but not all Python packages. To learn more, see the Pyodide
documentation.

In order for this file to be able to run, it must be served over HTTP,
and cannot be opened directly from the file system (e.g. file://).
"""
)
@click.option(
    "-o",
    "--output",
    type=click.Path(path_type=Path),
    required=True,
    help="Output directory to save the HTML to.",
)
@click.option(
    "--mode",
    type=click.Choice(["edit", "run"]),
    default="run",
    help="Whether the notebook code should be editable or readonly.",
    show_default=True,
    required=True,
)
@click.option(
    "--watch/--no-watch",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    help=("Whether to watch the original file and export upon change"),
)
@click.option(
    "--show-code/--no-show-code",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    help=(
        "Whether to show code by default in the exported HTML file; "
        "only relevant for run mode."
    ),
)
@click.option(
    "--include-cloudflare/--no-include-cloudflare",
    default=False,
    show_default=True,
    help=(
        "Whether to include Cloudflare Worker configuration files"
        " (index.js and wrangler.jsonc) for easy deployment."
    ),
)
@click.option(
    "--sandbox/--no-sandbox",
    is_flag=True,
    default=None,
    show_default=False,
    type=bool,
    help=_sandbox_message,
)
@click.argument(
    "name",
    required=True,
    type=click.Path(exists=True, file_okay=True, dir_okay=False),
)
def html_wasm(
    name: str,
    output: Path,
    mode: Literal["edit", "run"],
    watch: bool,
    show_code: bool,
    include_cloudflare: bool,
    sandbox: Optional[bool],
) -> None:
    """Export a notebook as a WASM-powered standalone HTML file."""
    import sys

    # Set default, if not provided
    if sandbox is None:
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox

        sandbox = maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox(name)

    if sandbox:
        from marimo._cli.sandbox import run_in_sandbox

        run_in_sandbox(sys.argv[1:], name=name)
        return

    out_dir = output
    filename = "index.html"
    ignore_index_html = False
    # If ends with .html, get the directory
    if output.suffix == ".html":
        out_dir = output.parent
        filename = output.name
        ignore_index_html = True

    marimo_file = MarimoPath(name)

    def export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult:
        return export_as_wasm(file_path, mode, show_code=show_code)

    # Export assets first
    Exporter().export_assets(out_dir, ignore_index_html=ignore_index_html)

    # Create .nojekyll file to prevent GitHub Pages from interfering with asset
    # resolution
    (Path(out_dir) / ".nojekyll").touch()

    echo(
        f"Assets copied to {green(str(out_dir))}. These assets are required for the "
        "notebook to run in the browser."
    )

    did_export_public = Exporter().export_public_folder(out_dir, marimo_file)
    if did_export_public:
        echo(
            f"The public folder next to your notebook was copied to "
            f"{green(str(out_dir))}."
        )

    echo(
        "To run the exported notebook, use:\n"
        f"  python -m http.server --directory {out_dir}\n"
        "Then open the URL that is printed to your terminal."
    )

    if include_cloudflare:
        create_cloudflare_files(parse_title(name), out_dir)

    outfile = out_dir / filename
    return watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), outfile, watch, export_callback)

summarization.summary:
### File Overview
This file defines the CLI "export" command group and several subcommands for exporting marimo notebooks to different formats (HTML, script, Markdown, Jupyter ipynb, and WASM-powered HTML). It centralizes export flow logic in watch_and_export, which writes export outputs, optionally prompts before overwriting, and can watch the source file for changes.

### Architecture
- Top-level click command group: `export` collects subcommands.
- Each subcommand (`html`, `script`, `md`, `ipynb`, `html_wasm`) defines an inner `export_callback` function that produces an `ExportResult` for a given `MarimoPath`, then calls the shared `watch_and_export` function with that callback.
- `watch_and_export` encapsulates:
  - validation (e.g., cannot use `--watch` without `--output`),
  - an overwrite prompt via `prompt_to_overwrite`,
  - a writer (`write_data`) that either writes to `output` or echoes to stdout,
  - a file-watching flow using `FileWatcher.create` and an async loop (`start`) to re-run the `export_callback` when the source changes.
- `html_wasm` has additional preparatory steps (copying assets using `Exporter().export_assets`, creating a `.nojekyll` file, exporting public folder via `Exporter().export_public_folder`, and optionally creating Cloudflare files via `create_cloudflare_files`) before delegating to `watch_and_export`.

The file uses utilities imported from the codebase such as `MarimoPath`, `ExportResult`, `export_as_*` functions, `run_app_then_export_*` functions, `asyncio_run`, `FileWatcher`, `prompt_to_overwrite`, and CLI helpers like `echo` and `green`.

### Functions & Classes
- `export` — a click group decorated function (click.group). Purpose: top-level command group for all export subcommands. Importance: organizes subcommands under `marimo export`.

- `watch_and_export` — signature: (marimo_path: MarimoPath, output: Optional[Path], watch: bool, export_callback: Callable[[MarimoPath], ExportResult]) -> None.
  - Overall purpose: central routine that executes an export once or repeatedly when watching a file, and writes/prints the export output.
  - Key behavior:
    - Raises `click.UsageError` if `watch` is True but `output` is not provided (ensures watching requires an output file).
    - Defines inner function `write_data` to either write the export data to `output` (creating parent dirs with `maybe_make_dirs`) or echo to stdout.
    - If `output` is provided, constructs `output_path = Path(output)` and calls `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)`. If `prompt_to_overwrite` returns False, `watch_and_export` returns early (no export occurs).
    - If `watch` is False: calls `export_callback(marimo_path)`, writes `result.contents` via `write_data`, raises `click.ClickException` if `result.did_error` is True (indicating some cells failed to execute), then returns.
    - If `watch` is True:
      - Defines async inner `on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None` that logs re-exporting and calls `export_callback(MarimoPath(file_path))` and writes the new `result.contents`.
      - Defines async inner `start() -> None` that creates a watcher via `FileWatcher.create(marimo_path.path, on_file_changed)`, starts the watcher, prints watching message, and waits in an infinite asyncio.sleep loop until KeyboardInterrupt, upon which it stops the watcher.
      - Runs the `start()` coroutine via `asyncio_run(start())`.
  - Importance: This is the central location where the overwrite prompt (`prompt_to_overwrite`) is invoked and where a force/skip-overwrite flag would need to be handled if introduced.

- `write_data` — inner function inside `watch_and_export` (exact name `write_data`).
  - Purpose: writes the export `data` string to `output` (creating directories via `maybe_make_dirs`) or echoes it to stdout if no `output` provided.
  - Importance: handles final persistence of exported contents; used both for single-run exports and re-exports during watch.

- `on_file_changed` — inner async function inside `watch_and_export` (exact name `on_file_changed`).
  - Signature: async def on_file_changed(file_path: Path) -> None
  - Purpose: callback invoked by the file watcher when the source file changes; logs and re-runs the `export_callback` for the changed file and writes the new contents via `write_data`.
  - Importance: drives re-export behavior when `--watch` is used.

- `start` — inner async function inside `watch_and_export` (exact name `start`).
  - Purpose: creates and starts the `FileWatcher` with `on_file_changed`, prints a watching message, and runs an infinite loop until KeyboardInterrupt to keep the CLI process alive while watching.
  - Importance: coordinates the lifetime of the file watcher and ensures the CLI remains responsive to file-change events.

- `html` — click command function: signature (name: str, include_code: bool, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool], args: tuple[str]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Run a notebook and export it as an HTML file."
  - Key behavior:
    - May prompt to run in a sandbox via `maybe_prompt_run_in_sandbox` if `sandbox` is None; if `sandbox` True, calls `run_in_sandbox`.
    - Parses extra CLI args via `parse_args(args)`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that calls `asyncio_run(run_app_then_export_as_html(...))`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: demonstrates how `export_callback` can run the app and generate HTML asynchronously; shows how `watch_and_export` is invoked for HTML exports.

- `script` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a marimo notebook as a flat script, in topological order."
  - Key behavior:
    - Optionally handles sandboxing similar to `html`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that returns `export_as_script(file_path)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: shows a simple synchronous `export_callback` that directly calls `export_as_script`.

- `md` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a marimo notebook as a code fenced Markdown file."
  - Key behavior:
    - Optionally handles sandboxing.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that returns `export_as_md(file_path, new_filename=output)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: illustrates passing `output` into the exporter (`new_filename=output`) and delegating to `watch_and_export`.

- `ipynb` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, watch: bool, sort: Literal["top-down", "topological"], include_outputs: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a marimo notebook as a Jupyter notebook in topological order."
  - Key behavior:
    - If `include_outputs` True, may prompt/perform sandboxing and may call `run_in_sandbox` with additional_deps `["nbformat"]`.
    - Requires `nbformat` via `DependencyManager.nbformat.require(...)`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult`:
      - If `include_outputs` True: returns `asyncio_run(run_app_then_export_as_ipynb(...))`.
      - Else: returns `export_as_ipynb(file_path, sort_mode=sort)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), output, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: shows conditional use of an async export path (when including outputs) vs synchronous exporter, and again delegates overwrite prompting and watching to `watch_and_export`.

- `html_wasm` — click command function: signature (name: str, output: Path, mode: Literal["edit", "run"], watch: bool, show_code: bool, include_cloudflare: bool, sandbox: Optional[bool]) -> None.
  - Purpose: CLI handler to "Export a notebook as a WASM-powered standalone HTML file."
  - Key behavior:
    - Optional sandbox prompt/run similar to other commands.
    - Determines `out_dir`, `filename`, and `ignore_index_html` depending on whether `output` ends with `.html`.
    - Prepares the output directory:
      - Calls `Exporter().export_assets(out_dir, ignore_index_html=ignore_index_html)`.
      - Creates a `.nojekyll` file in `out_dir`.
      - Logs asset copy messages via `echo` and `green`.
      - Calls `Exporter().export_public_folder(out_dir, marimo_file)` and logs if public folder copied.
      - If `include_cloudflare` True: calls `create_cloudflare_files(parse_title(name), out_dir)`.
    - Defines inner `export_callback(file_path: MarimoPath) -> ExportResult` that returns `export_as_wasm(file_path, mode, show_code=show_code)`.
    - Calls `watch_and_export(MarimoPath(name), outfile, watch, export_callback)`.
  - Importance: demonstrates pre-export asset copying and other side effects that occur before calling `watch_and_export`, and shows that `watch_and_export` will be used for re-exporting the main HTML file (the output passed is a concrete file path).

- `Exporter().export_assets` — method called in `html_wasm`. Purpose/importance here: copies required static assets to the output directory prior to exporting WASM HTML. (Referenced by exact name in code: `Exporter().export_assets`.)

- `Exporter().export_public_folder` — method called in `html_wasm`. Purpose/importance here: optionally copies a "public" folder next to the notebook into the output directory; the boolean return is checked and logged.

- `FileWatcher.create` — function/classmethod used in `watch_and_export` to create a watcher for the source path with callback `on_file_changed`. Importance: the mechanism used to watch file changes for `--watch`.

- `prompt_to_overwrite` — function imported from `marimo._cli.utils` and invoked in `watch_and_export` as `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)`.
  - Purpose/importance in this file: If `output` is provided, `watch_and_export` calls `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` and immediately returns (aborts the export) if it returns False. This is the single centralized prompt-before-overwrite behavior for all export commands in this file. Any change to add a `--force` flag to skip the prompt will need to alter how/where `prompt_to_overwrite` is invoked (e.g., add a flag parameter to `watch_and_export` and conditionally skip calling `prompt_to_overwrite`).

- Other referenced functions/utilities used here (exact names as used in file):
  - `maybe_make_dirs` — ensures output directories exist before writing files (used in `write_data`).
  - `echo`, `green` — CLI printing utilities used for messages.
  - `asyncio_run` — used to run async exporter operations and the watcher `start()` coroutine.
  - `run_app_then_export_as_html`, `run_app_then_export_as_ipynb`, `export_as_ipynb`, `export_as_md`, `export_as_script`, `export_as_wasm` — exporter functions used by various `export_callback` implementations.
  - `parse_args` — used in `html` to parse CLI args passed after `--`.
  - `create_cloudflare_files`, `parse_title` — used in `html_wasm` when `include_cloudflare` is True.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent wants to inspect `watch_and_export` and the usage of `prompt_to_overwrite` to plan modifications to accept a force flag. Key facts from this file that are relevant:
- `prompt_to_overwrite` is called exactly once in the file inside `watch_and_export` when `output` is truthy:
  - Code pattern: 
    - `if output:` -> `output_path = Path(output)` -> `if not prompt_to_overwrite(output_path): return`
- Because all export subcommands call `watch_and_export(...)`, adding a global `--force`/`--yes` style flag to skip overwrite prompts should be implemented by:
  - Adding a parameter (e.g., `force: bool`) to `watch_and_export`, and
  - Passing that parameter from each command (by adding a click option to the commands), and
  - Changing the prompt invocation to be conditional: only call `prompt_to_overwrite(output_path)` when `force` is False.
- The prompt is checked before any export (and before watch logic); the current behavior prevents any export if the user declines the prompt. Any change should preserve that guard unless `force` indicates to skip it.
- The watch-related logic is decoupled from prompting: `prompt_to_overwrite` is invoked once up front for the provided `output`, regardless of `watch`. So the force flag should be threaded through to `watch_and_export` rather than trying to modify each exporter callback.

This file is the correct central place to implement the force flag to bypass overwrite prompts for all export subcommands.

            line.startswith("Traceback")

def _delete_lines_with_files(output: str) -> str:
    return "\n".join(
        line for line in output.splitlines() if "File " not in line
    )

### File Overview
This file contains pytest-based CLI integration tests for the "marimo export" command across multiple export formats (html, html-wasm, script, md, ipynb). It exercises many CLI flags and behaviors (output paths, watch mode, sandbox, include-cloudflare, include-outputs, etc.) and asserts expected stdout/stderr, generated files, and return codes.

### Architecture
- Top-level helpers and imports set up fixtures and utilities: DependencyManager, normalize_index_html, snapshotter, and a small platform helper _is_win32.
- Tests are grouped by class, each class focusing on a particular export target or test-level grouping:
  - TestExportHTML: HTML and html-wasm related CLI tests.
  - TestExportHtmlSmokeTests: smoke/integration tests using marimo tutorial modules to validate exports produce files and expected error/non-error behavior.
  - TestExportScript: export to flat Python script tests.
  - TestExportMarkdown: export to Markdown tests.
  - TestExportIpynb: export to Jupyter Notebook tests (skipped when nbformat not present).
- Each test typically invokes subprocess.run or subprocess.Popen to call the "marimo" CLI and asserts return codes, stdout/stderr content, and/or filesystem side-effects (files created, copied, content inside index.html, .nojekyll presence, etc.).
- Helper functions at bottom cleanup/sanitize output: _delete_lines_with_files.

### Functions & Classes
- _is_win32 — small helper function that returns True if sys.platform == "win32". Used by pytest.skipif decorators for Windows-flaky tests.

Class TestExportHTML:
- test_cli_export_html — runs `marimo export html <file>`; asserts zero exit and that rendered HTML (normalized) does not include a hidden marimo-code placeholder.

class TestExportHtmlSmokeTests:
    def assert_not_errored(
        self, p: subprocess.CompletedProcess[bytes]
    ) -> None:
        assert p.returncode == 0, p.stderr.decode()
        assert not any(
            line.startswith("Traceback")
            for line in p.stderr.decode().splitlines()
        )
        assert not any(
            line.startswith("Traceback")
            for line in p.stdout.decode().splitlines()
        )

    def assert_has_errors(self, p: subprocess.CompletedProcess[bytes]) -> None:
        assert p.returncode != 0, p.stderr.decode()
        assert any(
            "Export was successful, but some cells failed to execute" in line
            for line in p.stderr.decode().splitlines()
        ), p.stderr.decode()
        assert not any(
            line.startswith("Traceback")
            for line in p.stdout.decode().splitlines()
        ), p.stdout.decode()

        p = subprocess.Popen(  # noqa: ASYNC101 ASYNC220
            [
                "marimo",
                "export",
                "script",
                temp_marimo_file,
                "--watch",
                "--output",
                temp_out_file,
            ],
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,

        assert p.poll() is None
        # Wait for rebuild

            if line:

        line for line in output.splitlines() if "File " not in line
